title: Crunchy Indian Sweet Rice Flour Vada
description: A funky and crunchy Indian dish made with sweet rice flour, perfect for crowds, this macrobiotic friendly vada is a must try. It is boiled and then fried to a golden brown.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the sweet rice flour, lentils, onions, ginger, green chilies, curry leaves, and salt.
Gradually add the water to the bowl and mix until a thick batter forms.
Heat the oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat.
Using a spoon, drop small balls of the batter into the hot oil.
Fry the vadas until they are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the vadas from the oil and drain on paper towels.
Serve the vadas hot with a side of chutney or sambar.
For an extra crunchy coating, boil the vadas in water for 5 minutes before frying.
To boil, bring a large pot of water to a boil and gently add the vadas.
Boil for 5 minutes, then remove with a slotted spoon and fry as instructed.
